key_insight: The paper develops a structure-preserving split scheme for compressible resistive Hall-MHD that separately handles fluid and magnetic dynamics while maintaining key physical invariants.
engineering_relevance: It offers a robust numerical tool for simulating plasma and magnetically dominated flows with better stability, conservation, and divergence control than standard methods.
method: Split the PDEs into hydrodynamic Euler and magnetic Lorentz/induction subproblems.
Use continuous Lagrange elements with SSP-RK for the fluid part and curl-conforming finite elements with Crank-Nicolson for the magnetic part.
Add high-order artificial resistivity to improve nonlinear conditioning and Newton solver invertibility.
evidence_signal: The method is validated on benchmark tests including a smooth whistler wave, the Orszag-Tang vortex, and magnetic reconnection, showing robustness and accuracy.
watch_item: The magnetic solve relies on Newton iterations and may need artificial resistivity tuning to ensure good conditioning and reliable convergence.
paper_title: A structure-preserving Numerical Method for the Compressible Resistive-Hall-MHD System
